devanagari: राजसूयान्तेऽवभृथस्नानमहोत्सवः मयनिर्मितायां युधिष्ठिरसभायां दुर्योधनस्यावमाननं च । श्रीराजोवाच । अजातशत्रोस्तं दृष्ट्वा राजसूयमहोदयम् ।...
english_devnagari: rājasūyānte śrīrājovāca ajātaśatrostaṃ dṛṣṭvā rājasūyamahodayam sarve mumudire brahman nṛdevā ye samāgatāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
King Parīkṣit enquired:
We have heard that all men, gods, kings and celestial beings who had assembled there for the Ṛājasūya sacrifice of king Ajātaśatru (Yudhiṣṭhira) were filled with joy to see its glorious accomplishment, O Brāhmaṇa sage. And Duryodhana was the only exception. Be pleased to explain to us. O worshipful sir, the reason of this.

devanagari: दुर्योधनं वर्जयित्वा राजानः सर्षयः सुराः । इति श्रुतं नो भगवन् तत्र कारणमुच्यताम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: duryodhanaṃ varjayitvā rājānaḥ sarṣayaḥ surāḥ iti śrutaṃ no bhagavan tatra kāraṇamucyatām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ śru class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ śru class 5 verb]

translation:
King Parīkṣit enquired:
We have heard that all men, gods, kings and celestial beings who had assembled there for the Ṛājasūya sacrifice of king Ajātaśatru (Yudhiṣṭhira) were filled with joy to see its glorious accomplishment, O Brāhmaṇa sage. And Duryodhana was the only exception. Be pleased to explain to us. O worshipful sir, the reason of this.

devanagari: श्रीबादरायणिरुवाच । पितामहस्य ते यज्ञे राजसूये महात्मनः । बान्धवाः परिचर्यायां तस्यासन् प्रेमबंधनाः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībādarāyaṇiruvāca pitāmahasya te yajñe rājasūye mahātmanaḥ bāndhavāḥ paricaryāyāṃ tasyāsan premabaṃdhanāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The sage Śuka replied.:
In the Rājasūya sacrifice of your illustrious grandfather, all his relatives attached to him with bonds of affection, accepted the responsibility of various departments and rendered him service (during the sacrifice).

devanagari: भीमो महानसाध्यक्षो धनाध्यक्षः सुयोधनः । सहदेवस्तु पूजायां नकुलो द्रव्यसाधने ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: bhīmo mahānasādhyakṣo dhanādhyakṣaḥ suyodhanaḥ sahadevastu pūjāyāṃ nakulo dravyasādhane
translation: Bhīma was the head of the culinary department, Duryodhana was in charge of treasury. Sahadeva was engaged in the reception department while Nakula was entrusted with, the provision of supplies (of things required).

devanagari: गुरुशुश्रूषणे जिष्णुः कृष्णः पादावनेजने । परिवेषणे द्रुपदजा कर्णो दाने महामनाः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: guruśuśrūṣaṇe jiṣṇuḥ kṛṣṇaḥ pādāvanejane pariveṣaṇe drupadajā karṇo dāne mahāmanāḥ
translation: Arjuna was. appointed to serve the elders and preceptors, while Kṛṣṇa was engaged in washing the feet (of the guests). Draupadī superintended service and catering of food while the noble-minded Karṇa was entrusted with conferring gifts.

devanagari: युयुधानो विकर्णश्च हार्दिक्यो विदुरादयः । बाह्लीकपुत्रा भूर्याद्या ये च सन्तर्दनादयः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: yuyudhāno vikarṇaśca hārdikyo vidurādayaḥ bāhlīkaputrā bhūryādyā ye ca santardanādayaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ yudh class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ yudh class 4 verb]
[nominative plural from √ ad ], [vocative plural from √ ad ]
[nominative plural from √ ad ], [vocative plural from √ ad ], [accusative plural from √ ad ]

translation: Yuyudhāna, Vikarṇa, Hārdikya, Vidura and others as well as Bhūri and other sons of Bāhlika, Santardana and others were entrusted with different departments and duties in that great sacrifice. And all of them endeavoured to carry out their duties, as liked by King Yudhiṣṭhira, O great King.

devanagari: निरूपिता महायज्ञे नानाकर्मसु ते तदा । प्रवर्तन्ते स्म राजेन्द्र राज्ञः प्रियचिकीर्षवः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: nirūpitā mahāyajñe nānākarmasu te tadā pravartante sma rājendra rājñaḥ priyacikīrṣavaḥ
translation: Yuyudhāna, Vikarṇa, Hārdikya, Vidura and others as well as Bhūri and other sons of Bāhlika, Santardana and others were entrusted with different departments and duties in that great sacrifice. And all of them endeavoured to carry out their duties, as liked by King Yudhiṣṭhira, O great King.

devanagari: ऋत्विक् सदस्यबहुवित्सु सुहृत्तमेषु । स्विष्टेषु सूनृतसमर्हणदक्षिणाभिः । चैद्ये च सात्वतपतेश्चरणं प्रविष्टे । चक्रुस्ततस्त्ववभृथ स्नपनं ...
english_devnagari: ṛtvik sadasyabahuvitsu suhṛttameṣu sviṣṭeṣu sūnṛtasamarhaṇadakṣiṇābhiḥ caidye ca sātvatapateścaraṇaṃ praviṣṭe cakrustatastvavabhṛtha snapanaṃ dyunadyām
translation: When the priests, member of the assembly, persons of wide and deep erudition, intimate friends and relatives were properly honoured with sweet words, costly presents and sacrificial (and other religious) fees and when (the soul of) Śiśupāla entered the feet of Lord Kṛṣṇa, the Pāṇḍavas took the closing ablution of the Rājasūya sacrifice in the heavenly river, Gaṅgā.

devanagari: मृदङ्गशङ्खपणव धुन्धुर्यानकगोमुखाः । वादित्राणि विचित्राणि नेदुरावभृथोत्सवे ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: mṛdaṅgaśaṅkhapaṇava dhundhuryānakagomukhāḥ vāditrāṇi vicitrāṇi nedurāvabhṛthotsave
translation: During the celebration of the ceremony of the avabhṛtha bath, various instruments of music such as tabors, conchs, paṇavas, drums, kettle-drums and horns were sounded.

devanagari: नर्तक्यो ननृतुर्हृष्टा गायका यूथशो जगुः । वीणावेणुतलोन्नादः तेषां स दिवमस्पृशत् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: nartakyo nanṛturhṛṣṭā gāyakā yūthaśo jaguḥ vīṇāveṇutalonnādaḥ teṣāṃ sa divamaspṛśat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ hṛṣ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ hṛṣ class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb]

[nominative plural from √ hṛṣ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ hṛṣ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ hṛṣ class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb]

translation: Dancing girls danced merrily. Bands after bands of songster engaged in vocal music. And sound of musical instruments like lutes, flutes and clapping reached the heaven.

devanagari: चित्रध्वजपताकाग्रैः इभेन्द्रस्यन्दनार्वभिः । स्वलङ्कृतैर्भटैर्भूपा निर्ययू रुक्ममालिनः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: citradhvajapatākāgraiḥ ibhendrasyandanārvabhiḥ svalaṅkṛtairbhaṭairbhūpā niryayū rukmamālinaḥ
translation: With their flags and streamers of variegated colours (fluttering in the air) and accompanied with battalions of well- adorned soldiers, big elephants, chariots and horses, kings wearing wreaths of gold started (for the avabhṛtha bath).

devanagari: यदुसृञ्जयकाम्बोज कुरुकेकयकोशलाः । कम्पयन्तो भुवं सैन्यैः यजनपुरःसराः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: yadusṛñjayakāmboja kurukekayakośalāḥ kampayanto bhuvaṃ sainyaiḥ yajanapuraḥsarāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ kamp ], [vocative plural from √ kamp ]

translation: With the sacrificer (Yudhiṣṭhira) in front of them followed the clans of Yādavas, Sṛñjayas, Kāmbojas, Kauravas. Kekayas and Kosalas shaking the earth with their armies.

devanagari: सदस्यर्त्विग्द्विजश्रेष्ठा ब्रह्मघोषेण भूयसा । देवर्षिपितृगन्धर्वाः तुष्टुवुः पुष्पवर्षिणः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: sadasyartvigdvijaśreṣṭhā brahmaghoṣeṇa bhūyasā devarṣipitṛgandharvāḥ tuṣṭuvuḥ puṣpavarṣiṇaḥ
translation: The members of the sacrificial assembly, the priests and prominent Brāhmaṇas chanting the Vedas loudly followed him. And the gods, sages, manes (ancestors) and celestial singers (Gandharvas) sang the praise of Yudhiṣṭhira and showered flowers (on the procession).

devanagari: स्वलंकृता नरा नार्यो गन्धस्रग् भूषणाम्बरैः । विलिंपन्त्यो अभिसिंच विजह्रुर्विविधै रसैः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: svalaṃkṛtā narā nāryo gandhasrag bhūṣaṇāmbaraiḥ viliṃpantyo abhisiṃca vijahrurvividhai rasaiḥ
translation: Men and women who have adorned themselves richly with clothes, ornaments, garlands and perfumes sported smearing and sprinkling each other with oil and saffron-mixed water.

devanagari: तैलगोरसगन्धोद हरिद्रासान्द्रकुंकुमैः । पुम्भिर्लिप्ताः प्रलिंपन्त्यो विजह्रुर्वारयोषितः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: tailagorasagandhoda haridrāsāndrakuṃkumaiḥ pumbhirliptāḥ praliṃpantyo vijahrurvārayoṣitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ lip class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ lip class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ lip class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ lip class 6 verb]

[nominative plural from √ lip class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ lip class 6 verb]

translation: The courtesans sported being smeared by men and reciprocally smearing men with oil, milk, perfumed water, turmeric powder and thick saffron paste.

devanagari: गुप्ता नृभिर्निरगमन्नुपलब्धुमेतद् । देव्यो यथा दिवि विमानवरैर्नृदेव्यः । ता मातुलेयसखिभिः परिषिच्यमानाः । सव्रीडहासविकसद् वदना विरेजुः...
english_devnagari: guptā nṛbhirniragamannupalabdhumetad devyo yathā divi vimānavarairnṛdevyaḥ tā mātuleyasakhibhiḥ pariṣicyamānāḥ savrīḍahāsavikasad vadanā virejuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ gup class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ gup class 4 verb]
[nominative plural from √ gup class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ gup class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ gup class 4 verb]
[imperfect active third plural], [aorist active third plural]
[nominative single from √ vikas class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vikas class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ vikas class 1 verb]

translation: With a desire to witness this procession and festival ladies of royal families ail well-escorted by guards went out in their vehicles (palanquins, chariots etc.) just as heavenly damsels crowded the sky in their excellent heavenly cars (to have a look at it). Those ladies when besprinkled by the friends of Śrī Kṛṣṇa (their maternal cousin) appeared still more beautiful with their faces blooming with bashful smiles.

devanagari: ता देवरानुत सखीन् सिन्सिषिचुर्दृतीभिः । क्लिन्नाम्बरा विवृतगात्रकुचोरुमध्याः । औत्सुक्यमुक्त कवरा च्च्यवमानमाल्याः । क्षोभं दधुर्मलधिय...
english_devnagari: tā devarānuta sakhīn sinsiṣicurdṛtībhiḥ klinnāmbarā vivṛtagātrakucorumadhyāḥ autsukyamukta kavarā ccyavamānamālyāḥ kṣobhaṃ dadhurmaladhiyāṃ rucirairvihāraiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ vac class 3 verb]
[vocative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ vac class 3 verb]

translation: With their syringes, the ladies of royal families sprinkled jets of coloured water on their brothers-in-law and friends. Their garments being moist, the limbs of their persons such as breasts, thighs, and hips were exposed to the view. In the excitement of watersport, the braids of their hair got loosened and flowers dropped down from them. By their attractive sportive activities, they provoked impious thoughts in impure- minded people.

devanagari: स सम्राड् रथमारुढः सदश्वं रुक्ममालिनम् । व्यरोचत स्वपत्‍नीभिः क्रियाभिः क्रतुराडिव ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: sa samrāḍ rathamāruḍhaḥ sadaśvaṃ rukmamālinam vyarocata svapat‍nībhiḥ kriyābhiḥ kraturāḍiva
translation: The emperor ascended the chariot drawn by excellent horses and decorated with wreaths of gold. Accompanied by his consorts he looked resplendent like the sovereign sacrifice Ṛājasūya with the attendant ritualistic processes incarnate.

devanagari: पत्‍नीसंयाजावभृथ्यैः चरित्वा ते तमृत्विजः । आचान्तं स्नापयां चक्रुः गंगायां सह कृष्णया ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: pat‍nīsaṃyājāvabhṛthyaiḥ caritvā te tamṛtvijaḥ ācāntaṃ snāpayāṃ cakruḥ gaṃgāyāṃ saha kṛṣṇayā
translation: After performing the special sacrifice called Patni- saṃyāja and other rituals connected with the concluding bath (avabhṛtha), king Yudhiṣṭhira performed ācamana (sipped water thrice from his right palm) and as directed by the priest, he along with Draupadī took bath in the Gaṅgā.

devanagari: देवदुन्दुभयो नेदुः नरदुंदुभिभिः समम् । मुमुचुः पुष्पवर्षाणि देवर्षिपितृमानवाः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: devadundubhayo neduḥ naraduṃdubhibhiḥ samam mumucuḥ puṣpavarṣāṇi devarṣipitṛmānavāḥ
translation: Drums from the heaven as well as those from the earth were simultaneously sounded. Gods, sages, manes and human beings showered flowers on them.

devanagari: सस्नुस्तत्र ततः सर्वे वर्णाश्रमयुता नराः । महापातक्यपि यतः सद्यो मुच्येत किल्बिषात् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: sasnustatra tataḥ sarve varṇāśramayutā narāḥ mahāpātakyapi yataḥ sadyo mucyeta kilbiṣāt

[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Thereupon, persons belonging to all classes of society (varṇas) and stages of life (āśrama) took their bath there; for even persons who perpetrated the most heinous sins are absolved of them by such a bath.

devanagari: अथ राजाहते क्षौमे परिधाय स्वलङ्कृतः । ऋत्विक् सदस्य विप्रादीन् आनर्चाभरणाम्बरैः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: atha rājāhate kṣaume paridhāya svalaṅkṛtaḥ ṛtvik sadasya viprādīn ānarcābharaṇāmbaraiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Then King Yudhiṣṭhira put on two new silk cloths and was well-adorned with ornaments. He then worshipped the sacrificial priests, the members of the (sacrificial) assembly, Brāhmaṇas and others by honouring them with clothes and ornaments

devanagari: बन्धूञ्ज्ञातीन् नृपान् मित्र सुहृदोऽन्यांश्च सर्वशः । अभीक्ष्णं पूजयामास नारायणपरो नृपः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: bandhūñjñātīn nṛpān mitra suhṛdo abhīkṣṇaṃ pūjayāmāsa nārāyaṇaparo nṛpaḥ
translation: The king who was unflinchingly devoted to Nārāyaṇa duly honoured his friends, kinsmen, kings, friends, well- wishers and all others (who were present) in all respects.

devanagari: सर्वे जनाः सुररुचो मणिकुण्डलस्रग् । उष्णीषकञ्चुक दुकूलमहार्घ्यहाराः । नार्यश्च कुण्डलयुगालकवृन्दजुष्ट । वक्त्रश्रियः कनकमेखलया विरेजुः...
english_devnagari: sarve janāḥ suraruco maṇikuṇḍalasrag uṣṇīṣakañcuka dukūlamahārghyahārāḥ nāryaśca kuṇḍalayugālakavṛndajuṣṭa vaktraśriyaḥ kanakamekhalayā virejuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [vocative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb]
[vocative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [vocative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb]

translation: Being adorned with jewelled ear-rings, wreaths of flowers, turbans, long coats, silk cloth and extremely costly necklaces, all men looked splendid like gods, while women, the beauty of whose countenance was enhanced by a pair of earrings and (overhanging) locks of hair, appeared charming with girdles of gold (round their waist).

devanagari: अथ ऋत्विजो महाशीलाः सदस्या ब्रह्मवादिनः । ब्रह्मक्षत्रियविट्शुद्रा राजानो ये समागताः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: atha ṛtvijo mahāśīlāḥ sadasyā brahmavādinaḥ brahmakṣatriyaviṭśudrā rājāno ye samāgatāḥ
translation: Thereupon highly virtuous priests (ṛtvijs), members of that assembly, the reciters of the Vedas, Brāhmaṇas, Kṣattriyās, Vaiśyas and Śūdras as well as the princes who assembled to attend the sacrifice; as also gods, sages, manes and other beings, the guardians of the world along with their followers were duly honoured. They sought the permission to go and returned to their respective regions.

devanagari: देवर्षिपितृभूतानि लोकपालाः सहानुगाः । पूजितास्तं अनुज्ञाप्य स्वधामानि ययुर्नृप ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: devarṣipitṛbhūtāni lokapālāḥ sahānugāḥ pūjitāstaṃ anujñāpya svadhāmāni yayurnṛpa

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ pūj class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ pūj class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ pūj class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ pūj class 10 verb]

[nominative plural from √ pūj class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ pūj class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ pūj class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ pūj class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ pūj class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ pūj class 10 verb]

translation: Thereupon highly virtuous priests (ṛtvijs), members of that assembly, the reciters of the Vedas, Brāhmaṇas, Kṣattriyās, Vaiśyas and Śūdras as well as the princes who assembled to attend the sacrifice; as also gods, sages, manes and other beings, the guardians of the world along with their followers were duly honoured. They sought the permission to go and returned to their respective regions.

devanagari: हरिदासस्य राजर्षे राजसूयमहोदयम् । नैवातृप्यन् प्रशंसन्तः पिबन् मर्त्योऽमृतं यथा ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: haridāsasya rājarṣe rājasūyamahodayam naivātṛpyan praśaṃsantaḥ piban martyo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pā class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ pā class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ pā class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ pā class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ pā class 1 verb]

translation: Even as a mortal being is not sated by drinking the nectar, people did not feel satiated by glorifying the splendid success of the Rājasūya sacrifice performed by that royal sage Yudhiṣṭhira who was but a humble servant of Lord Hari.

devanagari: ततो युधिष्ठिरो राजा सुहृत् संबंन्धि बान्धवान् । प्रेम्णा निवारयामास कृष्णं च त्यागकातरः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: tato yudhiṣṭhiro rājā suhṛt saṃbaṃndhi bāndhavān premṇā nivārayāmāsa kṛṣṇaṃ ca tyāgakātaraḥ
translation: King Yudhiṣṭhira could not bear the idea of separation and affectionately importuned his friends, relatives, kith and kin and Lord Kṛṣṇa to stay longer.

devanagari: भगवानपि तत्राङ्ग न्यवात्सीत् तत्प्रियंकरः । प्रस्थाप्य यदुवीरांश्च साम्बादींश्च कुशस्थलीम् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavānapi tatrāṅga nyavātsīt tatpriyaṃkaraḥ prasthāpya yaduvīrāṃśca sāmbādīṃśca kuśasthalīm
translation: Dear Parīkṣit! The Lord despatched Sāmba and other Yādava warriors to Dvārakā. Being desirous of pleasing king Yudhiṣṭhira, the Lord continued to stay on with him.

devanagari: इत्थं राजा धर्मसुतो मनोरथमहार्णवम् । सुदुस्तरं समुत्तीर्य कृष्णेनासीद् गतज्वरः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: itthaṃ rājā dharmasuto manorathamahārṇavam sudustaraṃ samuttīrya kṛṣṇenāsīd gatajvaraḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]

translation: In this way, with the help of Lord Kṛṣṇa, king Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Dharma, crossed over the ocean of his cherished ambition (of performing the Rājasūya sacrifice) which was very difficult to be accomplished. And he was thus thoroughly cured of his fever of anxiety for proper performance of the Rājasūya.

devanagari: एकदान्तःपुरे तस्य वीक्ष्य दुर्योधनः श्रियम् । अतप्यद् राजसूयस्य महित्वं चाच्युतात्मनः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: ekadāntaḥpure tasya vīkṣya duryodhanaḥ śriyam atapyad rājasūyasya mahitvaṃ cācyutātmanaḥ
translation: On a certain occasion, Duryodhana was sorely troubled at heart to notice the glorious splendour of king Yudhiṣṭhira’s harem and the grandeur of the Rājasūya (and the eminent position attained thereby) by Yudhiṣṭhira whose heart was devoted to Lord Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: यस्मिन् नरेन्द्रदितिजेन्द्र सुरेन्द्रलक्ष्मीः । नाना विभान्ति किल विश्वसृजोपकॢप्ताः । ताभिः पतीन्द्रुपदराजसुतोपतस्थे । यस्यां विषक्तहृ...
english_devnagari: yasmin narendraditijendra surendralakṣmīḥ nānā vibhānti kila viśvasṛjopakḷptāḥ tābhiḥ patīndrupadarājasutopatasthe yasyāṃ viṣaktahṛdayaḥ kururāḍatapyat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: In that harem, all the affluence and royal splendour of kings of men, demon kings and Lord of gods accumulated and utilised by Maya, the architect of that palace, were shining resplendently. With all these, Draupadī, the princess of king Drupada, waited upon her spouses. The heart of Duryodhana, king of Kauravas, was attached to her and hence he was tormented (with jealousy).

devanagari: यस्मिन् तदा मधुपतेर्महिषीसहस्रं । श्रोणीभरेण शनकैः क्वणदङ्घ्रिशोभम् । मध्ये सुचारु कुचकुङ्कुमशोणहारं । श्रीमन्मुखं प्रचलकुण्डलकुन्तलाढ...
english_devnagari: yasmin tadā madhupatermahiṣīsahasraṃ śroṇībhareṇa śanakaiḥ kvaṇadaṅghriśobham madhye sucāru kucakuṅkumaśoṇahāraṃ śrīmanmukhaṃ pracalakuṇḍalakuntalāḍhyam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kvaṇ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kvaṇ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kvaṇ class 1 verb]

translation: At that time, thousands of consorts of Lord Kṛṣṇa were staying in that palace. Owing to the heaviness of their buttocks, they had to walk slowly and the anklets on their feet jingled sweetly. They were slender-waisted, and their (white) pearl necklaces became reddish with saffron applied to their breasts. And their beautiful countenances appeared still more attractive with their rocking ear-rings and curly locks of hair.

devanagari: सभायां मयकॢप्तायां क्वापि धर्मसुतोऽधिराट् । वृतोऽनुगैर्बन्धुभिश्च कृष्णेनापि स्वचक्षुषा ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: sabhāyāṃ mayakḷptāyāṃ kvāpi dharmasuto vṛto

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]

translation: Once, in a part of that assembly constructed by Maya, emperor Yudhiṣṭhira, surrounded by his younger brothers and relatives and by Kṛṣṇa who was his eye (indicating what is in his interest or otherwise), was seated on a throne of gold like Indra, the Lord of gods himself. He was endowed with imperial glory and was glorified by bards.

devanagari: आसीनः काञ्चने साक्षात् आसने मघवानिव । पारमेष्ठ्यश्रीया जुष्टः स्तूयमानश्च वन्दिभिः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: āsīnaḥ kāñcane sākṣāt āsane maghavāniva pārameṣṭhyaśrīyā juṣṭaḥ stūyamānaśca vandibhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb]

translation:
Once, in a part of that assembly constructed by Maya, emperor Yudhiṣṭhira, surrounded by his younger brothers and relatives and by Kṛṣṇa who was his eye (indicating what is in his interest or otherwise), was seated on a throne of gold like Indra, the Lord of gods himself. He was endowed with imperial glory and was glorified by bards.
[Padaratnāvalī’s Text adds:[
35(A). He was waited upon by prominent Brāhmaṇas coming from different parts of the country. Fanned with many white coloured chowries, the tiger among the kings (emperor Yudhiṣṭhira) shone like the Lord of gods in the heaven.

devanagari: तत्र दुर्योधनो मानी परीतो भ्रातृभिर्नृप । किरीटमाली न्यविशद् असिहस्तः क्षिपन् रुषा ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: tatra duryodhano mānī parīto bhrātṛbhirnṛpa kirīṭamālī nyaviśad asihastaḥ kṣipan ruṣā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṣip class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ kṣip class 6 verb]

translation: There entered the swaggering proud Duryodhana adorned with a crown and garlands with a sword in his hand. He was surrounded, by his brothers and he was angrily railing at the guards at the door and others.

devanagari: स्थलेऽभ्यगृह्णाद् वस्त्रान्तं जलं मत्वा स्थलेऽपतत् । जले च स्थलवद् भ्रान्त्या मयमायाविमोहितः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: sthale jale ca sthalavad bhrāntyā mayamāyāvimohitaḥ
translation:
He was so much deluded by the skill of Maya that when there was a part of the floor, he mistook it for a sheet of water and drew up his clothes and fell on the floor and at places tumbled into water mistaking it (the sheet of water) as floor.
[Padaratnāvalī’s Text adds:]
37(A). He regarded the floor paved with sapphires as a pond of water and took up his clothes and all people laughed at him.
37(B). When king Duryodhana was proceeding to a pond of water, he thought it to be crystal pavement and fell into that pond.

devanagari: जहास भीमस्तं दृष्ट्वा स्त्रियो नृपतयोऽपरे । निवार्यमाणा अप्यङ्ग राज्ञा कृष्णानुमोदिताः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: jahāsa bhīmastaṃ dṛṣṭvā striyo nṛpatayo nivāryamāṇā apyaṅga rājñā kṛṣṇānumoditāḥ

translation: O dear Parīkṣit. Seeing that Bhīma laughed at him; so too laughed the ladies and other kings even though king Yudhiṣṭhira tried to check them. Kṛṣṇa approved of them.

devanagari: स व्रीडितोऽवाग्वदनो रुषा ज्वलन् । निष्क्रम्य तूष्णीं प्रययौ गजाह्वयम् । हाहेति शब्दः सुमहानभूत् सतां । अजातशत्रुर्विमना इवाभवत् । बभूव...
english_devnagari: sa vrīḍito niṣkramya tūṣṇīṃ prayayau gajāhvayam hāheti śabdaḥ sumahānabhūt satāṃ ajātaśatrurvimanā ivābhavat babhūva tūṣṇīṃ bhagavānbhuvo bharaṃ samujjihīrṣurbhramati sma yad dṛśā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vrīḍ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ vrīḍ class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ vrīḍ class 10 verb]
[nominative single from √ jval class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ jval class 1 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Duryodhana felt abashed and he hung down his head. As if burning with rage, he silently left for Hastināpura. There was a great uproar (of dismay and regrets) among the pious members of the assembly. The foe-less king Yudhiṣṭhira was dejected in mind. The Lord who was bent on reducing the burden of the earth, remained silent. It was through his deluding potency that Duryodhana was led into these mistakes.[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
According to Bhāvāratha Dīpikā, this delusion of Duryodhana was the ‘seed’ of re­moving the burden of the earth and Bhīma’s laughter indicated Duryodhana’s discomfiture. Hence, Kṛṣṇa kept quiet.

devanagari: एतत्तेऽभिहितं राजन् यत्पृष्टोऽहमिह त्वया । सुयोधनस्य दौरात्म्यं राजसूये महाक्रतौ ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: etatte suyodhanasya daurātmyaṃ rājasūye mahākratau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation:
O king, I have narrated to you what you asked me how Duryodhana’s wickedness developed during the great sacrifice of Rājasūya.
[back to top]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे उत्तरार्धे । दुर्योधनमानभंगो नाम पञ्चसप्ततितमोऽध्यायः ॥ ७५ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe uttarārdhe duryodhanamānabhaṃgo nāma pañcasaptatitamo
